arguments.query: Layered architecture pattern Snipara layer hierarchy "Layer Hierarchy" "Snipara"

tool_results.content:
{'sections': [{'title': 'Layer Hierarchy', 'content': '### Layer Hierarchy

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  app/api/          Routes HTTP (THIN handlers)      │  ↓ Can import
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  lib/jobs/         Orchestrators (processors)       │  ↓ Can import
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  lib/services/     External API adapters            │  ↓ Can import
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  lib/db/queries    Repository (centralized CRUD)    │  ↓ Can import
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  types/, utils/    Shared (NO dependencies)         │  ✗ Imports nothing
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Rules:**

- Lower layers CANNOT import from higher layers
- `types/` and `utils/` have NO dependencies on other layers
- Database queries are ONLY in `lib/db/queries/`
', 'file': 'CLAUDE.md', 'lines': [14081, 14101], 'relevance_score': 1.0, 'token_count': 227, 'truncated': False}, {'title': '.snipara.toml — Single source of truth for Snipara + rlm-runtime', 'content': '# .snipara.toml — Single source of truth for Snipara + rlm-runtime

[project]
api_key = "rlm_..."
project_slug = "my-project"
api_url = "https://api.snipara.com"       # optional, defaults to production

[context]
max_tokens = 4000
search_mode = "hybrid"                     # keyword | semantic | hybrid
include_shared_context = true
shared_context_budget_percent = 30

[runtime]
backend = "litellm"
model = "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
environment = "local"                      # local | docker | wasm
max_depth = 6
max_subcalls = 20
token_budget = 12000
verbose = true

[runtime.docker]                           # only used when environment = "docker"
image = "python:3.11-slim"
cpus = 2.0
memory = "1g"
timeout = 300

[sync]
watch = true                               # enable file watcher for event-driven sync
patterns = ["docs/**/*.md", "CLAUDE.md"]   # glob patterns to watch
ignore = ["node_modules", ".git", "dist"]
debounce_ms = 2000                         # debounce rapid changes
```

**Migration path:**

- rlm-runtime reads `.snipara.toml` → `[project]` + `[runtime]` sections
- snipara-mcp reads `.snipara.toml` → `[project]` + `[context]` sections
- `.mcp.json` continues to work for Claude Code/Cursor MCP server discovery (unchanged)
- `rlm.toml` is deprecated but still read as fallback with a warning

**Config resolution order:**
1. Environment variables (`SNIPARA_API_KEY`, etc.)
2. `.snipara.toml` in current directory (walk up to git root)
3. `~/.config/snipara/config.toml` (global default)
```

response:
Snipara uses a strict layered architecture with well‑defined layers and one‑way dependencies (higher layers may import lower layers, but lower layers must not import higher layers).

Layers (top → bottom)
- app/api/ — HTTP route handlers (thin handlers)
- lib/jobs/ — orchestrators / processors
- lib/services/ — external API adapters
- lib/db/queries — repository layer (centralized CRUD)
- types/, utils/ — shared code with no dependencies

Key rules (from the docs)
- Lower layers CANNOT import from higher layers.
- types/ and utils/ must have NO dependencies on other layers.
- Database queries must live only in lib/db/queries/.
